title: Git node Clone/Push source and target repository paths bypass N8N_RESTRICT_FILE_ACCESS_TO sandbox
description: The Git node resolves and sandbox-checks only the repositoryPath (the working directory) against N8N_RESTRICT_FILE_ACCESS_TO via resolvePath/isFilePathBlocked, but it never validates the sourceRepository used by the Clone operation nor the targetRepository used by the Push operation. In the Clone branch the user-supplied sourceRepository is passed to git.clone() with no path-blocking check, so an authenticated user able to create or edit workflows can specify a local filesystem path (or file:// URL) pointing at any git repository readable by the n8n process. The repository is cloned into an allowed working directory and its contents become readable, circumventing the file-access sandbox that correctly blocks direct reads of the same paths; the Push operation is similarly affected via its target repository.
